source.title: OISD-STD-105 — Work Permit System
source.authority: Oil Industry Safety Directorate (OISD), Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas, Government of India
source.provenance: OISD standards are mandatory for the hydrocarbon (oil & gas) sector. Their permit-to-work, confined-space and hot-work provisions are widely adopted as good engineering practice across high-hazard process facilities, including coke-oven gas (COG) handling. Cited here by analogy for the COG complex; clause texts are faithful summaries, not verbatim.
source.note: Defines the four permit classes — cold work, hot work, confined space / vessel entry, and electrical isolation — and the precautions each requires. References are to named provisions of the standard rather than sub-clause numbers.

clauses.title: Documented permit-to-work with cross-checking of simultaneous jobs
clauses.summary: All non-routine maintenance in a hazardous area shall be controlled by a documented work permit that identifies the job, the hazards, and the precautions, and is authorised by a competent issuer. The issuer must account for other simultaneous jobs in the vicinity so that one activity does not create a hazard for another; permits and their conditions must be communicated across shift handovers.

clauses.ref: Sec. Confined Space / Vessel Entry Permit
clauses.title: Positive isolation, purging, gas testing and standby before entry
clauses.summary: Entry into a confined space or vessel is permitted only after the equipment is positively isolated from all connected sources (blinding / spading / double-block-and-bleed), drained and purged, and gas-tested (hydrocarbons in %LEL, toxic gases in ppm, oxygen in %). A trained standby person shall be posted at the entry, and gas monitoring shall continue for the duration of the entry.

clauses.title: Gas tests must represent the whole space and be repeated
clauses.summary: Gas tests shall be representative of the entire confined space, including remote and rear pockets where heavier-than-air or trapped gas can accumulate, not a single point at the entrance. Testing shall be repeated at a defined frequency and after any interruption to the work or change in conditions.

clauses.title: Gas test below LEL and control of flammable sources before hot work
clauses.summary: Hot work near hydrocarbon or flammable sources requires a gas test confirming the atmosphere is below the lower explosive limit, removal or isolation of nearby flammable sources, a posted fire watch, and continuous surveillance. Hot work shall not proceed where a flammable release is credible in the surrounding area.

clauses.title: Positive isolation — a closed valve alone is not isolation
clauses.summary: Lines carrying hazardous fluids to equipment under maintenance or confined-space entry shall be positively isolated (blinding / spading / double-block-and-bleed). A single throttled or closed valve is not positive isolation, because it can pass, and throttling can raise back-pressure on the isolated leg.
